idea-plugin.name: Context Condenser - LVM
idea-plugin.description:
🧊 Context Condenser — LVM

Cut LLM token costs by 70–90% without leaving your IDE.

Stop paying to send the same boilerplate to Claude, GPT-4, or Gemini every prompt. Context Condenser uses a Python AST parser to compress your code into structural skeletons — function signatures only — and only expands the exact functions the AI needs, exactly when it needs them.

Features

  • AST-Aware Skeletonization — Uses Python’s ast module to preserve function signatures, type hints, and docstring summaries while condensing bodies to ...
  • 3-Tier Condensation — Tier 1: strip comments | Tier 2: structural AST (default) | Tier 3: AI-driven prose summary
  • Live Token Badge — Real-time display of raw tokens vs condensed tokens with percentage saved
  • Dual Scope Modes — Toggle between Current File (micro-context) and Full Project (architectural map) from the tool window
  • Smart Navigation — Double-click any def or class line in the condensed view to jump directly to the source in your editor
  • One-Click AI Copy — Ctrl+Shift+Alt+C copies a formatted LLM prompt with context header to your clipboard
  • Jupyter Support — Condenses .ipynb code cells, strips output cells
  • Project Skeleton — Ctrl+Shift+Alt+P generates a full Markdown architectural map of your entire codebase with token budget guard
  • Auto-Index on Save — Re-indexes the active file in under 50ms every time you save

Requirements

  • Python 3.8+ configured as the project interpreter
  • Run Tools → Context Condenser: Setup Python Environment to install tiktoken for exact token counts (optional — falls back to estimation)

Context Condenser 2026

action.description: Skeletonize current Python file and copy LLM prompt to clipboard
Condense all Python files into a single architectural map
Install tiktoken into the project Python interpreter
